nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: Birria tacos provide substantial protein from the slow-braised beef. The cooking method concentrates flavor, which can increase sodium and fat depending on preparation; trim excess fat before braising and skim fat from the consommé to reduce saturated fat. Serving with corn tortillas limits added refined carbs; add a side salad or beans for fiber and extra micronutrients. Use moderate portion sizes and lime/onion/cilantro instead of heavy cheese or crema to keep the meal balanced.

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Slow-cook shredded beef (or a beef-goat blend) with dried chiles, onion, garlic, and warming spices until tender and flavorful. Shred the meat and use the cooking broth (consommé) for dipping. Warm corn tortillas, dip briefly in the rendered fat or consommé, fill with shredded birria, and pan-fry until slightly crisp. Serve topped with chopped onion, cilantro, and a lime wedge, alongside a small bowl of consommé for dipping.

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
